# tidycomm

Tidycomm provides convenience functions for common tasks in
communication research. All functions follow the style and syntax of the
[tidyverse](https://www.tidyverse.org/).

Currently, tidycomm includes functions for various methods of univariate
and bivariate data description and analysis, data modification,
visualization, and intercoder reliability tests.

## Installation

Install tidycomm from CRAN:

``` r

install.packages("tidycomm")
```

Or install the most recent development version of tidycomm with:

``` r

remotes::install_github("joon-e/tidycomm")
```

## Usage

``` r

library(tidycomm)
```

tidycomm functions follow the style and syntax of the
[tidyverse](https://www.tidyverse.org/) functions:

- they always assume a `tibble` as their first argument
- they will always return a `tibble` as well, so they can be easily
  integrated into pipes
- data variables (`tibble` columns) are passed to function calls
  directly as symbols

``` r

WoJ %>% # Worlds of Journalism sample data
  describe(autonomy_selection, autonomy_emphasis)
#> # A tibble: 2 × 15
#>   Variable             N Missing     M    SD   Min   Q25   Mdn   Q75   Max Range
#> * <chr>            <int>   <int> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
#> 1 autonomy_select…  1197       3  3.88 0.803     1     4     4     4     5     4
#> 2 autonomy_emphas…  1195       5  4.08 0.793     1     4     4     5     5     4
#> # ℹ 4 more variables: CI_95_LL <dbl>, CI_95_UL <dbl>, Skewness <dbl>,
#> #   Kurtosis <dbl>
```

Most functions will automatically use all relevant variables in the data
if no variables are specified in the function call. For example, to
compute descriptive statistics for all numeric variables in the data,
just call
[`describe()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/describe.md)
without further arguments:

``` r

WoJ %>% 
  describe()
#> # A tibble: 11 × 15
#>    Variable           N Missing     M     SD   Min   Q25   Mdn   Q75   Max Range
#>  * <chr>          <int>   <int> <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>
#>  1 autonomy_sele…  1197       3  3.88  0.803     1  4        4     4     5     4
#>  2 autonomy_emph…  1195       5  4.08  0.793     1  4        4     5     5     4
#>  3 ethics_1        1200       0  1.63  0.892     1  1        1     2     5     4
#>  4 ethics_2        1200       0  3.21  1.26      1  2        4     4     5     4
#>  5 ethics_3        1200       0  2.39  1.13      1  2        2     3     5     4
#>  6 ethics_4        1200       0  2.58  1.25      1  1.75     2     4     5     4
#>  7 work_experien…  1187      13 17.8  10.9       1  8       17    25    53    52
#>  8 trust_parliam…  1200       0  3.05  0.811     1  3        3     4     5     4
#>  9 trust_governm…  1200       0  2.82  0.854     1  2        3     3     5     4
#> 10 trust_parties   1200       0  2.42  0.736     1  2        2     3     4     3
#> 11 trust_politic…  1200       0  2.52  0.712     1  2        3     3     4     3
#> # ℹ 4 more variables: CI_95_LL <dbl>, CI_95_UL <dbl>, Skewness <dbl>,
#> #   Kurtosis <dbl>
```

Likewise, compute intercoder reliability tests for all variables by only
specifying the post and coder ID variables:

``` r

fbposts %>% # Facebook post codings sample data
  test_icr(post_id, coder_id)
#> # A tibble: 5 × 8
#>   Variable     n_Units n_Coders n_Categories Level   Agreement Holstis_CR
#> * <chr>          <int>    <int>        <int> <chr>       <dbl>      <dbl>
#> 1 type              45        6            4 nominal     1          1    
#> 2 n_pictures        45        6            7 nominal     0.822      0.930
#> 3 pop_elite         45        6            6 nominal     0.733      0.861
#> 4 pop_people        45        6            2 nominal     0.778      0.916
#> 5 pop_othering      45        6            4 nominal     0.867      0.945
#> # ℹ 1 more variable: Krippendorffs_Alpha <dbl>
```

Almost all functions also come with integrated visualizations. So to
visualize your results you can easily just append
[`visualize()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/visualize.md)
to the end of your commands:

``` r

snscomments %>% # experimental social-media-post perception sample data
  dplyr::filter(group == 3) %>% 
  t_test(gender, 
         medium_evaluation, 
         article_evaluation, 
         comments_quality, 
         comments_valence) %>% 
  visualize()
```

![](reference/figures/README-unnamed-chunk-8-1.png)

For detailed examples, see the following articles:

- [Univariate analysis of continuous and categorical
  variables](https://joon-e.github.io/tidycomm/articles/v01_univariate.html)
- [Bivariate analysis of continuous and/or categorical
  variables](https://joon-e.github.io/tidycomm/articles/v02_bivariate.html)
- [Adding indices and computing reliability
  estimates](https://joon-e.github.io/tidycomm/articles/v03_reliability.html)
- [Intercoder reliability
  tests](https://joon-e.github.io/tidycomm/articles/v04_icr.html)
- [Modification of
  scales](https://joon-e.github.io/tidycomm/articles/v05_scales.html)

# Package index

## Univariate analysis

- [`describe()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/describe.md)
  : Describe numeric variables
- [`tab_percentiles()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/tab_percentiles.md)
  : Tabulate percentiles for numeric variables
- [`describe_cat()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/describe_cat.md)
  : Describe categorical variables
- [`tab_frequencies()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/tab_frequencies.md)
  : Tabulate frequencies
- [`knit_frequencies()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/knit_frequencies.md)
  : knit frequencies table variables

## Bivariate analysis

- [`crosstab()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/crosstab.md)
  : Crosstab variables
- [`knit_crosstab()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/knit_crosstab.md)
  : knit crosstab table variables
- [`t_test()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/t_test.md)
  : Compute t-tests
- [`unianova()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/unianova.md)
  : Compute one-way ANOVAs
- [`correlate()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/correlate.md)
  : Compute correlation coefficients
- [`to_correlation_matrix()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/to_correlation_matrix.md)
  : Create correlation matrix
- [`regress()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/regress.md)
  : Compute linear regression
- [`knit_regress_table()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/knit_regress_table.md)
  : Compute linear regression

## Data modification

- [`add_index()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/add_index.md)
  : Add index
- [`reverse_scale()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/reverse_scale.md)
  : Reverse numeric, logical, or date/time continuous variables
- [`minmax_scale()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/minmax_scale.md)
  : Rescale numeric continuous variables to new minimum/maximum
  boundaries
- [`center_scale()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/center_scale.md)
  : Center numeric, continuous variables
- [`z_scale()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/z_scale.md)
  : Z-standardize numeric, continuous variables
- [`categorize_scale()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/categorize_scale.md)
  : Categorize numeric variables into categories
- [`dummify_scale()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/dummify_scale.md)
  : Convert categorical variables to dummy variables
- [`setna_scale()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/setna_scale.md)
  : Set specified values to NA in selected variables or entire data
  frame
- [`recode_cat_scale()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/recode_cat_scale.md)
  : Recode one or more categorical variables into new categories

## Reliability tests

- [`get_reliability()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/get_reliability.md)
  : Get reliability estimates of index variables

## Intercoder reliability

- [`test_icr()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/test_icr.md)
  : Perform an intercoder reliability test

## Visualization

- [`visualize()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/visualize.md)
  : Visualize tidycomm output
- [`design_lmu()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/design_lmu.md)
  : Colorbrewer-inspired design with focus on LMU (lmu.de) green
- [`design_uzh()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/design_uzh.md)
  : Colorbrewer-inspired design with focus on UZH (uzh.ch) blue
- [`design_viridis()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/design_viridis.md)
  : viridis-inspired design
- [`design_gray()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/design_gray.md)
  : Gray design
- [`design_grey()`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/design_grey.md)
  : Grey design

## Sample data

- [`WoJ`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/WoJ.md) :
  Worlds of Journalism sample data
- [`fbposts`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/fbposts.md)
  : Facebook posts reliability test
- [`snscomments`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/snscomments.md)
  : SNS Comments data
- [`incvlcomments`](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/reference/incvlcomments.md)
  : Incivil Comments Data

# Articles

### All vignettes

- [Univariate analysis of continuous and categorical
  variables](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/articles/v01_univariate.md):
- [Bivariate analysis of continuous and/or categorical
  variables](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/articles/v02_bivariate.md):
- [Adding indices and computing reliability
  estimates](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/articles/v03_reliability.md):
- [Intercoder reliability
  tests](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/articles/v04_icr.md):
- [Change
  Scales](https://github.com/tidycomm/tidycomm/articles/v05_scales.md):
